title: eriogonum wrightii var. brevifolium
intro: Eriogonum wrightii var. brevifolium, also known as the short-leaved Mormon tea or Wright's buckwheat, is a perennial shrub that belongs to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). Traditionally, the leaves and stems of this plant have been used for medicinal purposes, particularly for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum wrightii var. brevifolium include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and providing relief from cold and flu symptoms. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues.
